page: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, utilizing 48 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 2880 GB NVMe SSD. The instance also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong performance characteristics. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top tier, while multi-core performance ranks in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier write speeds for large blocks, though large-block read-only bandwidth remains in the average tier. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform exceptionally well, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both reaching top-tier status. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, consistently rank in the top 25%.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads that require substantial memory and storage without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. The combination of a 2880 GB NVMe SSD and 192.0 GB of RAM makes it highly suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference tasks, where text generation speeds for small and medium models reach top-tier levels. However, the shared CPU model presents a potential performance tradeoff under sustained, simultaneous multi-core utilization.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2880 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write speeds, alongside strong multi-core processing and database operation capabilities. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention under sustained loads, the high memory-to-core ratio and substantial local NVMe storage offer a cost-effective platform for database hosting, static web serving, and text-generation workloads.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server featuring 48 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 2880 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 2880 GB NVMe SSD for database and web workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance combining forty-eight shared AMD EPYC cores with substantial NVMe storage and balanced memory allocation.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
